expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the case focus and then separates coverage into contextual-link styling vs ordinary rendering: “Modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering.” It provides distinct sections: “A) Contextual-link styling (hyperlink affordance)” and “B) Ordinary rendering (non-link elements must not look like links)”, plus “C) Mixed-state separation (links vs non-links in same grid)” to ensure no styling bleed.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md declares “Primary phase under test - phase4a” and frames the output as “Phase4a-aligned draft coverage to include”. It references Phase 4a contract constraints and claims compliance: “Per Phase 4a contract (subcategory-only; no canonical top-layer categories)...” and “Stays at subcategory → scenario → atomic action chain → observable verification leaves; Avoids forbidden canonical top categories...” The structure presented is subcategory headings with scenario bullets, consistent with that phase4a description.
